critic_raw:
Issue RAD-1 — Simulation evidence does not support outcome improvement claim.
Issue RAD-2 — Downstream workflow steps from read to treatment outcome are unmodeled.
Issue RAD-3 — Simulation is not deployment; compliance assumption is an upper bound.
defender_raw:
Pass 1 (Critic output visible):
The pattern across all three issues is consistent with the previous runs. RAD-1: mechanism is plausible but evidence is not deployment-quality. RAD-2: the downstream workflow is the key empirical unknown. RAD-3: concede.

Pass 2: RAD-1 empirically open; RAD-2 empirically open; RAD-3 conceded. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

debate_rounds.critic_move: Accept empirical test for both. The mechanism for RAD-1 is plausible — the critic does not contend the tool is without value, only that the outcome claim needs deployment validation. RAD-2 is the key test: if time-to-treatment does not reduce in deployment, the worklist reordering benefit is not reaching patients.
debate_rounds.defender_move: Agreed. Empirical test: prospective deployment measuring time-to-read, time-to-treatment, and parallel escalation rate. If time-to-treatment reduction is confirmed, the outcome claim is validated. If not, the memo's framing needs to be downgraded to 'reduces time-to-read, outcome benefit contingent on workflow.'
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: all three issues — RAD-3 conceded, RAD-1 and RAD-2 empirical test agreed

adjudication_raw:
ADJUDICATION — scope_intent_radiology_queue_302 — Multi-Round Run 3

All three runs produce the same result: simulation-not-deployment conceded, outcome and downstream workflow questions empirically open and resolvable by prospective deployment study.

Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED

empirical_test.measure: prospective deployment: time-to-read for urgent studies; time-to-treatment; parallel escalation rate
empirical_test.success_criterion: time-to-read and time-to-treatment reduce; outcome signal in urgent-finding cohort vs. pre-deployment baseline
empirical_test.failure_criterion: time-to-read improves but time-to-treatment unchanged; model benefit absorbed by downstream bottleneck
